summary_one_line: iTXt chunk with a length field large enough that png_read_buffer's allocation fails and returns NULL.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed PNG prelude (signature + IHDR) followed by an iTXt chunk whose 4-byte big-endian length header is set to a value large enough that the subsequent png_read_buffer(length+1, 1) call cannot allocate it (png_malloc_base returns NULL), forcing the True branch at pngrutil.c:2748. The input does not need to actually contain `length` bytes of chunk data — only the declared length field matters because the allocation is attempted before the data is read.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Both winners (naive and value_profile_cmplog) reach the buffer==NULL True branch in 8/10 trials. The winner-resolving seeds we have are 8-12 KB sized PNGs whose declared iTXt chunk length is large enough to defeat png_malloc_base; the per-role overlay shows W exclusively executing the OOM-handling block (lines 2749-2753) and never the buffer!=NULL post-buffer-read path, confirming buffer == NULL fires.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 1/10 trials and is blocked 9/10 with avg duration 10.80h. Its I2SRandReplace mutator, driven by CmpLogObserver entries on the chunk-length and chunk-type CMPs that fire during chunk-header parsing, systematically substitutes the length field back to plausible in-file values (or to constants seen in length CMPs upstream), pinning length to ranges that png_malloc_base can satisfy. The L seeds in BRANCH SEEDS take the False branch and continue into the prefix-scanning loop at L2761-L2764 and beyond — i.e., they reach the chunk with a length the allocator handled.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Composite Pattern-B story (claimed_mechanism = other; no single positive enabler explains both decisive pairs). On (naive, cmplog) the input-to-state substitution mutator that cmplog carries on top of naive's stack is counterproductive here: it scans CmpLogObserver entries from chunk-length and chunk-type comparisons and pulls the 4-byte iTXt length field back toward plausible recorded values, undoing the random-large mutations that naive's pure havoc/token stack produces freely. On (value_profile_cmplog, cmplog) the value-profile Hamming-distance feedback signal rewards progress on the allocation-size comparison path independently of the input-to-state substitutions that trap cmplog. Both decisive pairs collapse to the same single feature requirement (a huge declared iTXt length), so the technique label `other` is the honest pick — it is the I2S sub-mutator hurting cmplog that drives the divergence, not any single positive enabler shared by the two winners.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is `if (buffer == NULL)` at pngrutil.c:2748 in OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_iTXt and the globally blocked side is True.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Location: /src/libpng/pngrutil.c:2748:8
Enclosing function: OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_iTXt
Source line:    if (buffer == NULL)
Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Buffer is assigned from png_read_buffer(png_ptr, length+1, 1) immediately before the blocking branch, so taking the True branch requires that allocation to fail.
Winners execute only the OOM-handling block (lines 2749-2753) and never the buffer-consumption path that follows; losers execute the post-allocation path including the prefix-scanning loop.
naive resolves 8/10 trials while cmplog resolves 1/10 and is blocked 9/10; value_profile_cmplog also resolves 8/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            8        2          0  winner (I2S vs cmplog)
cmplog                           1        9          0  loser (I2S vs naive); loser (value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog)
value_profile                    8        2          0  REFERENCE
value_profile_cmplog             8        2          0  winner (value_profile vs cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs identify cmplog as the loser with the same prob_div=0.70 and the same 1/10 vs 8/10 split; both pair-level deltas are statistically significant.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
--- Pair 1: naive > cmplog  [delta: I2S] ---
  subject 9  (cmplog vs naive, admissible)
  winner: resolved=8/10  blocked=2  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0
  avg duration blocked: winner=4.70h  loser=10.80h
  avg hitcount on branch: winner=1  loser=0
  prob_div=0.70  dur_div=6.10h  hit_div=1
  subject-level: delta_AUC=3983370.0  p_AUC=0.0002  delta_Final=309.8  p_final=0.0002
--- Pair 2: value_profile_cmplog > cmplog  [delta: value_profile] ---

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds proceed past the blocker into the keyword-prefix scanning loop, confirming they had a length the allocator handled.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2762  T=0 F=0  T=75 F=2  prefix_length < length && buffer[prefix_length] != 0;

evidence_trail.claim: naive's mutator stack is pure havoc/token with no I2S — it cannot CMP-anchor mutations on the chunk-length field.

cmplog's I2SRandReplace scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other, which on chunk-length CMPs pulls a havoc-mutated huge length back toward plausible recorded values.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog has both CMP_MAP gradient and I2S; the CMP_MAP gradient bucket signal lets it climb the allocation/length CMP gradient even when I2S substitutions push back.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Instrumentation**: union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Claimed mechanism is `other` (Pattern-B technique-hurts on (cmplog, naive)): the winner's enabling mechanism is naive's pure havoc on the 4-byte chunk-length field, not I2SRandReplace; the loser cmplog underperforms because I2S substitution on chunk-length CMPs anchors the length back to plausible values. As a corroborating check I still ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7423 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 4 --seed 62c6887c9402b9b5` on cmplog's single winning seed; that chain does contain one I2S-floor dash row at depth 8, but that is the cmplog-specific lineage of cmplog's lucky 1/10 win and does not explain naive's 8/10 advantage. The per-direction seed_bisect picked cmplog/trial=4 lex-min so no naive winner seed is in resolving_seeds to lineage-verify the naive=havoc-only claim directly; the naive-mechanism attribution rests on the documented mutator stack in MECHANISM CONTEXT, not on a per-seed lineage trace.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A parameterized synthetic that gates a `buffer == NULL` branch behind a 4-byte big-endian length field at a fixed file offset, swept over scan_values = {0x00000100, 0x00100000, 0x10000000, 0xFFFFFFFF}, should show cmplog's resolve rate decreasing monotonically as the required length grows and diverging from naive's by >=30 percentage points at the highest scan value. If instead cmplog matches or beats naive at the high-length scan points, the I2S-anchoring story is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly inspect the 4-byte iTXt length field in the winner seeds because BYTE DIFF only shows the first 64 bytes and the iTXt length header sits deeper in the file; the chunk-length-anchoring story is inferred from the source allocation path plus mutator-stack semantics rather than from a visible W-vs-L byte difference at the length offset.
